key_insight: Danus scales research-level mathematical reasoning by coordinating multiple agents around a shared fact graph that stores verified claims, proofs, and dependencies as global memory.
engineering_relevance: This architecture offers a practical blueprint for long-horizon proof search systems that need parallel exploration, reliable state tracking, and human-in-the-loop oversight.
method: Main agent plans and redirects search across worker agents.
Workers generate proof attempts in parallel.
A stateless verifier admits only checked facts into the shared fact graph.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports six research-level case studies spanning algebraic geometry, singularity theory, and combinatorics to illustrate long proof construction.
watch_item: Performance may depend on verifier quality and on maintaining a consistent fact graph as proofs become very large and interdependent.
paper_title: Danus: Orchestrating Mathematical Reasoning Agents with Fact-Graph Memory
